data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Nordson FY25 outlook falls below expectations, while FQ4 tops estimates
Mid-America Apartment Communities announces pricing of senior unsecured notes offering
data.description:
• Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA) announced that its operating partnership, Mid-America Apartments, L.P. priced a $350M offering of its's 4.9% senior unsecured notes due March 1, 2035, under its existing shelf registration statement.
  • The notes were priced at 99.170% of the principal amount.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on December 18, 2024.
  • Mid-America Apartments, L.P. intends to use net proceeds from the offering to repay borrowings outstanding under its unsecured commercial paper program, with any remaining net proceeds to be used for general corporate purposes, which may include, the repayment of other debt and the acquisition, development and redevelopment of apartment communities.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Blackstone Secured Lending prices public offering of $300 million 5.350% unsecured notes
data.description:
• Blackstone Secured Lending Fund (NYSE:BXSL) on Wednesday said it has priced an underwritten public offering of $300 million in aggregate principal amount of 5.350% notes due 2028.
  • The notes will mature on April 13, 2028, and may be redeemed in whole or in part at BXSL’s option at the applicable redemption price.
  • The notes will be a further issuance of the company’s 5.350% notes due 2028 that the company issued on October 15, 2024, in an aggregate principal amount of $400 million.
  • Upon the issuance of the notes, the company will have $700.0 million aggregate principal amount of 5.350% notes due 2028 outstanding.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 16, 2024.
  • BXSL +0.52% after hours to $32.25.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nordson Non-GAAP EPS of $2.78 beats by $0.19, revenue of $744M beats by $7.21M
data.description:
• Nordson press release (NASDAQ:NDSN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.78 beats by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $744M (+3.4% Y/Y) beats by $7.21M.

  • Full Year:

  • Record sales of $2.7 billion, reflecting 2% growth over last year’s record sales

  • Earnings per diluted share were $8.11

  • EBITDA was a record $849 million, up 4% over prior year and 32% of sales

  • Adjusted earnings per diluted share were $9.73

  • Fiscal 2025 Guidance:

  • Fiscal 2025 forecasted sales range between $2,750 to $2,870 million and adjusted earnings in the range of $9.70 to $10.50

data.title: Oxford Industries' Q3 sales suffer, leading to lowered FY24 guidance
data.description:
Led by lower sales across all its major labels due to the cumulative effects of several years of high inflation and distractions from the election and global events, Oxford Industries (NYSE:OXM) swung to a loss in the third quarter as sales were down 6% for the combined company, both of which missed Wall Street’s expectations and drove shares more than 6% lower in after-hours trading.

With the added impact from two major hurricanes in the company’s most significant market and a highly competitive and promotional environment, “these headwinds led to financial performance that was weaker than expected,” said CEO Tom Chubb.

And as a result of these events, the parent company of Tommy Bahama, Lilly Pulitzer, and Johnny Was lowered its FY24 sales and EPS guidance.

The company now expects to earn a profit between $6.50 and $6.70 per share on sales of $1.50B to $1.52B. This was lowered from $7.00 to $7.30 per share on $1.51B to $1.54B in revenue, and is below the consensus estimate of $7.06 per share on $1.52B in revenue.

For Q4, Oxford (OXM) expects earnings to be between $1.18 and $1.38 per share on sales of $375M to $395M. This compares to Q4 2023 EPS of $1.90 on $404M, and to the consensus estimates of $1.55 per share on $389.25M in sales.

For the reported quarter, sales were down 8.5% for Lilly Pulitzer, down 6.1% for Johnny Was, and down 5.2% for Tommy Bahama, resulting in total sales of $308.0M which missed expectations by $8.83M. By channel, DTC sales were down 8%, wholesale sales were down 2%, while outlet sales increased 3%. The loss in sales resulted in a loss for the quarter of $0.11 per share, compared to a profit of $1.01 a year ago, and $0.20 below expectations. Gross margin, however, improved 20 basis points to 63.1%, largely due to a $4M lower LIFO accounting charge and lower discounts at Lilly Pulitzer.

Finally, the company’s board said it will pay a cash dividend of $0.67 per share on January 31 to shareholders of record as of January 17.

data.title: WesBanco, Premier Financial shareholders vote to approve merger
data.description:
The shareholders of both WesBanco (NASDAQ:WSBC) and Premier Financial (NASDAQ:PFC) voted to adopt and approve all proposals needed for WesBanco to acquire Premier, the companies said Wednesday.

In July, the WesBanco (WSBC) agreed to acquire Premier (PFC) in an all-stock transaction that valued Premier at ~$959M at that time.

About 85% of the votes cast at the WesBanco (WSBC) special meeting approved the merger and the proposal to issue WesBanco common stock as described in the prospectus for the special meeting. Also, ~68% of outstanding shares of Premier (PFC) were voted in favor of the merger agreement.

With that milestone achieved, the companies believe the merger is on track to close during Q1 2025. The transaction remains subject to the completion of customary closing conditions, including the receipt of required regulatory approvals.

More on Wesbanco

  • WesBanco Non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.05, revenue of $150.75M misses by $1.92M

  • WesBanco files to sell 7.27M shares of common stock for holders
